category_title: At-risk Grade 9 students exhibiting learned helplessness and inconsistent engagement
minimal_context: The student is working on a worksheet calculating wattage and power in science class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please complete the worksheet on calculating wattage and power.

STUDENT: I don't see why we have to do this. It's completely disconnected from my daily life. I'd rather just learn how circuits are wired into a house.

TUTOR: Just focus on the calculation problems in front of you.

STUDENT: (Stares at the paper) The mathematical aspect of calculating this wattage is too challenging.

TUTOR: Try your best to figure it out.

STUDENT: There's no point. I'm just stupid, so I'm always going to fail these tests anyway. I'm not doing it.

options.A: Implement science activities with less demanding rules for the wattage calculations to be utilized by the student throughout the science class sessions.
options.B: Implement a progressive disciplinary sequence during the science class sessions: first a warning, then a 10-minute timeout, followed by classroom suspension, detention, or school suspension.
options.C: Provide opportunities for the student to move from learned helplessness to high self-efficacy by experiencing personal success on the wattage calculations, alongside encouragement and praise.
options.D: Provide pictorial descriptions of the mathematical problems for the wattage calculations to be utilized by the student throughout the science class sessions.
